summary: The frontend container implements a component‑based Angular architecture organized into presentation, business‑logic, and infrastructure layers and communicates with the backend via REST APIs. It delivers a comprehensive set of legal‑deed management capabilities, from creation and workflow handling to reporting and user settings. Quality metrics indicate moderate separation of concerns, coupling, and cohesion, resulting in a grade of C. The primary technical debt stems from an excessive number of services, adapters, and pipes, and a lack of documented module relationships, which together threaten maintainability and scalability.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, Guards, Interceptors)
Infrastructure/Adapter Layer (Adapters, HTTP Interceptors, API Modules)

capabilities: Create, view, edit, approve and delete deeds
Manage deed workflows (finalize, validate signatures, reencryption)
Import and export document data (JSON, PDF, etc.)
Generate reports and metadata for deeds
Manage tasks, jobs and number assignments
Provide UI components for data entry, validation and correction notes
functional.bounded_context: Legal Deed Management and Workflow Processing

debt_indicators: Excessive number of services (131) suggesting possible God services
High count of adapters (50) indicating potential abstraction leakage
Large number of pipes (67) may lead to duplication and maintenance overhead
Lack of recorded component/module relations makes dependency analysis difficult
recommendations: Consolidate related services into feature‑scoped modules or facade services to reduce surface area
Introduce a clear adapter/factory abstraction layer to limit direct adapter usage across the app
Review and merge similar pipes; consider utility functions or shared pipe libraries
Document module boundaries and enforce dependency rules (e.g., via lint rules or architecture tests)
Limit the proliferation of adapters by defining generic adapter interfaces where appropriate
Implement architectural guardrails (e.g., layer enforcement, bounded context segregation) to improve separation of concerns

overall_assessment: The frontend delivers rich functional coverage and follows a sensible Angular component model, but its architectural health is hampered by service, adapter, and pipe bloat as well as undocumented module dependencies. Addressing these quality issues is essential to sustain future development velocity and system stability.
top_recommendations: Consolidate related services into feature‑scoped modules or facade services
Introduce a clear adapter/factory abstraction layer to curb adapter proliferation
Document module boundaries and enforce dependency rules via lint or architecture tests

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, Guards, Interceptors)
Infrastructure/Adapter Layer (Adapters, HTTP Interceptors, API Modules)
